title: CV
text: I am a data-intensive empirical researcher trained in economic geography and regional innovation, with interests in high-skilled mobility, urban and regional development, and innovation networks. My work emphasizes longitudinal multi-source data construction, spatial and network analysis, applied empirical design, and measurement-validity audits.

title: Projects
text:
Working Paper

Research question. How is a scientist’s pre-move publication-location exposure associated with observed destination choice among candidate cities?
Data or material. I work with event-specific destination-choice data derived from elite biomedical scientist mobility records. Restricted source data are not public.
Methods. Event-specific candidate sets, origin-anchored pre-move exposure measures, conditional logit models, and candidate-set and coverage diagnostics.
Current status. This is a working paper. I do not publicly release the restricted source data or the full manuscript materials here.

title: Projects
section: Global Mobility and Collaboration Networks of Elite Biomedical Scientists
text:
Global Mobility and Collaboration Networks of Elite Biomedical Scientists

Master’s Thesis; accepted article; ongoing research extension

Master’s thesis. My master’s thesis examines the intercity mobility network of top global biomedical scientists from whole-network and ego-network perspectives.
Accepted article. A related Chinese article studies the spatial structure and determinants of top global biomedical scientists’ mobility from an urban network perspective. I do not list final publication metadata until it is confirmed.
Ongoing extension. My current extension builds longitudinal scientist-institution-city data, directed weighted mobility networks, ego-network indicators, and WERGM-based specifications. Raw data are not public.

title: Projects
section: Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit
text:
Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit

Public Code Repository

Research question. How can author-institution-city linkage and geocoding workflows be audited before restricted bibliographic data are used for research on scientific geography or mobility?
Data or material. The public repository uses fully synthetic demonstration records. It does not include real Scopus, OpenAlex, ROR, scientist, paper, or institution data.
Methods. Schema validation, identifier linkage, deterministic institution normalization, city-country validation, record-level QA, duplicate and confidence diagnostics, and coverage reporting.
Links. https://github.com/Olivia-wr-research/author-institution-city-linkage

title: Research
text: I study how elite scientist mobility, publication-location exposure, collaboration networks, and career trajectories are related to the spatial organization of scientific work. I treat scientist mobility as a relational and temporally ordered process, connecting individual career histories with city-level and institutional networks while carefully distinguishing observed affiliations, employment histories, collaboration ties, and mobility events.

title: Research
text: I study how elite scientist mobility, publication-location exposure, collaboration networks, and career trajectories are related to the spatial organization of scientific work. I treat scientist mobility as a relational and temporally ordered process, connecting individual career histories with city-level and institutional networks while carefully distinguishing observed affiliations, employment histories, collaboration ties, and mobility events.

title: Research
text:
Urban and Regional Innovation
I also study urban opportunity structures, regional innovation, scientific and technological platforms, and talent concentration. This work asks how cities and regions accumulate research capacity, organizational diversity, and knowledge-network positions, and how those conditions are associated with innovation and high-skilled mobility. My emphasis is on careful empirical measurement rather than causal claims beyond the available design.

title: Research
text:
Computational and Spatial Methods
I work with longitudinal multi-source data, conditional logit models, ERGM/WERGM, spatial and network analysis, entity resolution, and measurement audits. I am especially interested in reproducible workflows for building scientist-institution-city data, auditing source coverage, and making the unit of analysis explicit before applying statistical or network models.

title: Home
text:
Applied Empirical Research | Urban & Regional Economics |
High-Skilled Mobility and Innovation


I study how scientist mobility, knowledge networks, and urban innovation systems are related to the spatial organization of research and innovation. My work combines longitudinal multi-source data construction with spatial, network, and applied empirical methods, with a focus on high-skilled mobility, regional innovation, and measurement-validity audits for complex relational data.


Research Projects Download my CV GitHub

title: Home
text:
Current focus
I work at the intersection of economic geography, urban and regional economics, science of science, and computational social science. I am particularly interested in how career trajectories, institutional platforms, collaboration relations, and city-level opportunity structures can be measured with defensible longitudinal data.

title: Home
text:
Featured public project
Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit is a public code repository that demonstrates synthetic author-institution-city linkage, schema validation, linkage and geocoding QA, duplicate and confidence diagnostics, and reproducible documentation. It does not redistribute real Scopus, OpenAlex, or restricted source data.
